raw_brief:
PURPOSE
pixiedining.com is a paid subscription service that sends instant push, SMS, and email alerts when hard-to-book Disney World restaurants get cancellation slots. A visitor arriving on the homepage should be able to understand what the alerts do, see proof that the alert actually fires fast on a real phone, and pay for a $6.99/month subscription without leaving the page. The pricing page is the secondary surface where a $49/year annual plan is upsold.

AUDIENCE
The first paying customer is an individual Disney trip planner — not an agency or concierge. The founder considered targeting both but committed to the individual planner as the primary persona. This is a person who has already tried to book a specific hard-to-get Disney restaurant (the founder's own example is Beak and Barrel) and failed repeatedly. Disney's 60-day booking window plus its no-show penalty creates a constant churn of last-minute cancellations, and this audience is the one refreshing reservation pages by hand to try to catch them. They are cold, skeptical, and price-sensitive but the price point ($6.99/month, cheaper than a coffee) is low enough that the decision risk is small.

CONTEXT
The founder's own pain grounds the offering: he checked for a Beak and Barrel slot more than 50 times across days and never landed it. He tried DreamTable, a competing alert service, and found alerts arrived 10-15 minutes late and push notifications were broken. Mouse Dining, another competitor, charges per alert. pixiedining.com's wedge is two-fold: instant alerts (vs. DreamTable's 10-15 minute lag) and flat subscription pricing that is cheaper than per-alert competitors.

The primary acquisition channel at launch is the founder personally dropping helpful comments in Reddit (r/WaltDisneyWorld) and Facebook Disney-planning groups when someone vents about not landing a reservation. This means visitors arrive cold and skeptical from a single contextual link, often having been previously burned by DreamTable. SEO, Instagram, and TikTok content are planned but explicitly secondary to comment-driven traffic for the first 50 paying users.

KEY ACTIONS
The single primary action on the homepage is clicking the $6.99/month CTA and entering payment. There is no free trial and no free first alert — the founder explicitly rejected a freemium leap-reducer in favor of a direct CTA-to-payment flow at a price low enough to absorb the risk itself. The pricing page is where the $49/year annual plan is offered as an upsell once the visitor has shown intent.

The trust anchor above the fold is a phone-screen recording showing an alert hitting a lock screen seconds after a cancellation drops — chosen by the founder over a live public ticker as the more believable proof in three seconds. The aesthetic should feel playful and magical, in the spirit of Disney parks blogs the audience already reads (warm colors, whimsical typography), rather than a sterile utility-first SaaS look. The page must not emit untrustworthy signals — the visitor has likely been burned by a buggy competitor and any sign of amateurism kills the conversion.
optional_business_context: B2C self-serve subscription. $6.99/month unlimited alerts is the primary CTA; $49/year is the upsell on the pricing page after a visitor clicks through. No free trial, no free first alert — direct CTA to payment. Monetization depends on retention through a Disney trip cycle plus annual conversion.
optional_known_constraints: Visitors arrive cold and skeptical from Reddit/Facebook comments, often previously burned by DreamTable. Page must not emit untrustworthy signals. Promise is sub-15-minute (effectively instant) alerts via push, SMS, and email. Disney-restaurant niche only at launch; Beak and Barrel is the named anchor restaurant.
optional_user_preferences: Playful and magical aesthetic in the spirit of Disney parks blogs — warm colors, whimsical typography — explicitly not a stripped-down utility-first SaaS look. Trust anchor above the fold is a phone-screen recording of an alert firing on a lock screen, not a live ticker.
optional_existing_site_or_brand_notes: Domain is pixiedining.com. Competitors named as foils: DreamTable (buggy, 10-15 min late alerts, broken push) and Mouse Dining (per-alert pricing). No existing brand assets mentioned.
